sacco.gui

Class SFrame

  • java.lang.Object
    • sacco.gui.SFrame
  • Direct Known Subclasses:
    HandCheckerRunner

    public class SFrame
extends java.lang.Object

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      SFrame()
      Constructs an SFrame object with no title
      SFrame(java.lang.String title)
      Constructs an SFrame object with the given title

    • Method Summary

      Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void add(SButton sButton)
      Adds the SButton to the SFrame
      void add(SComponent s) 
      void add(SLabel sLabel)
      Adds the sLabel to the SFrame
      void add(SPanel sPanel)
      Adds the SPanel to the SFrame
      void add(SPasswordField field)
      Adds the SPasswordField to the SFrame
      void add(STextArea sArea)
      Adds the STextArea to the SFrame
      void add(STextField field)
      Adds the STextField to the SFrame
      void add(java.lang.String direction, SButton sButton)
      Adds the given SButton at the given direction.
      void add(java.lang.String direction, SComponent s) 
      void add(java.lang.String north, SLabel sLabel) 
      void add(java.lang.String direction, SPanel sPanel)
      Adds the given SPanel at the given direction.
      void add(java.lang.String direction, STextArea textArea)
      Adds the given STextArea at the given direction.
      void add(java.lang.String direction, STextField textField)
      Adds the given STextArea at the given direction.
      void centerOnScreen()
      Moves the Frame to be centered on the screen
      void exitOnClose()
      After calling the exitOnClose method, closing the SFrame will end the program and stop all other SFrames.
      javax.swing.JFrame getSwing()
      Not designed for normal student use.
      java.lang.String getTitle()
      Returns the current title of the SFrame
      void pack()
      Automatically resizes the window to the natural size of the components in the SFrame
      void setBackgroundColor(Color color)
      Sets the background color of the frame to the given color
      void setLayout(LayoutManager manager)
      Sets the layout manager for the SFrame.
      void setLocation(int x, int y)
      Sets the location of the window to the given coordinates
      void setMenuBar(SMenuBar bar) 
      void setResizable(boolean b)
      Sets the resizability of the SFrame.
      void setSize(int width, int height)
      Sets the size of the SFrame's window to the given width and height
      void setTitle(java.lang.String title)
      Sets the title of the SFrame to the given String
      void setVisible(boolean b)
      Sets the visibility of the SFrame's window.

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        equals, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

    • Constructor Detail

      • SFrame

        public SFrame()
        Constructs an SFrame object with no title

      • SFrame

        public SFrame(java.lang.String title)
        Constructs an SFrame object with the given title
        Parameters:
        title - the title of the frame

    • Method Detail

      • exitOnClose

        public void exitOnClose()
        After calling the exitOnClose method, closing the SFrame will end the program and stop all other SFrames. This is a necessary step when creating executable jar files. Otherwise the process will continue running in the background and can cause problems

      • setLayout

        public void setLayout(LayoutManager manager)
        Sets the layout manager for the SFrame. Layout managers include FLowLayout, BorderLayout, or GridLayout
        Parameters:
        manager -

      • setTitle

        public void setTitle(java.lang.String title)
        Sets the title of the SFrame to the given String
        Parameters:
        title - The title of the SFrame

      • setSize

        public void setSize(int width,
           int height)
        Sets the size of the SFrame's window to the given width and height
        Parameters:
        width - the new width of the SFrame
        height - the new height of the SFrame

      • setBackgroundColor

        public void setBackgroundColor(Color color)
        Sets the background color of the frame to the given color
        Parameters:
        color -

      • getTitle

        public java.lang.String getTitle()
        Returns the current title of the SFrame
        Returns:
        the title of the SFrame

      • getSwing

        public javax.swing.JFrame getSwing()
        Not designed for normal student use. It returns the wrapped JFrame object.
        Returns:

      • add

        public void add(java.lang.String direction,
       SButton sButton)
        Adds the given SButton at the given direction. Directions include "NORTH", "SOUTH","EAST", "WEST", or "CENTER". This method is only for BorderLayouts and will throw an IllegalArgumentException if currently not.
        Parameters:
        direction - the Section of the BorderLayout to put the SButton
        sButton - the SButton to add

      • add

        public void add(java.lang.String direction,
       STextField textField)
        Adds the given STextArea at the given direction. Directions include "NORTH", "SOUTH","EAST", "WEST", or "CENTER". This method is only for BorderLayouts and will throw an IllegalArgumentException if currently not.
        Parameters:
        direction - the Section of the BorderLayout to put the SButton
        textField - the STextField to add

      • add

        public void add(java.lang.String direction,
       STextArea textArea)
        Adds the given STextArea at the given direction. Directions include "NORTH", "SOUTH","EAST", "WEST", or "CENTER". This method is only for BorderLayouts and will throw an IllegalArgumentException if currently not.
        Parameters:
        direction - the Section of the BorderLayout to put the SButton
        textField - the STextField to add

      • add

        public void add(java.lang.String direction,
       SPanel sPanel)
        Adds the given SPanel at the given direction. Directions include "NORTH", "SOUTH","EAST", "WEST", or "CENTER". This method is only for BorderLayouts and will throw an IllegalArgumentException if currently not.
        Parameters:
        direction - the Section of the BorderLayout to put the SButton
        sPanel - the SPanel to add

      • setVisible

        public void setVisible(boolean b)
        Sets the visibility of the SFrame's window.
        Parameters:
        b - true to make the SFrame visible, false if not

      • setLocation

        public void setLocation(int x,
               int y)
        Sets the location of the window to the given coordinates
        Parameters:
        x -
        y -

      • centerOnScreen

        public void centerOnScreen()
        Moves the Frame to be centered on the screen

      • setResizable

        public void setResizable(boolean b)
        Sets the resizability of the SFrame.
        Parameters:
        b - false means the SFrame is not resizable

      • pack

        public void pack()
        Automatically resizes the window to the natural size of the components in the SFrame
